Eight years ago the Spokane Gas
Light company was quiet, inof-
fensive corporation, carrying on its
business in an easy, everyday sort
of manner, taking its profits at a
stiff rate for the service rendered,
but not otherwise imposing on the
dear public in the way Of taking
it's money for watered stock or
questionable bond issues.


Era Of the Promoter.


Then came the era of the pro-
moter. The first contingent to ar
rive on the scene were the repSe-
sentatives of N. W. Halsey & Co.
and Cyrus Pierce 4 Co., who had
acquired all but eight shares OF
the stock of the Spokane Falls
Gas Light Co. They waNted a
new franchise in order to better
carry on their operations. They
did not want a new gas company,
but new franchise, so the city
council of that day was appealed to,
